demiplane.
HomeDocsAPIConnectGalleryHelpllms.txt
Works with your harness

Connect demiplane

demiplane publishes from any coding harness, agent, or shell. Pick your tool below and copy the block. Every snippet targets this instance (https://demiplane.example) and each publish returns a URL only your network can reach.

This instance is open (no bearer token configured). The snippets below work as-is; if the operator later enables auth, save your token to ~/.config/demiplane/token and the same blocks keep working.

Compatibility

HarnessHow it connects
Claude CodeMCP (native tools) or capture hook
CursorMCP
ClineMCP
WindsurfMCP
ZedMCP
ContinueMCP
Aidershell /run + curl
Any shell / CIcurl or demiplane publish

MCP harnesses

Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, Zed, and Continue all speak the Model Context Protocol. Register demiplane once and publish, list, get, and delete become native tools. The stanza is the same everywhere; only the config file location differs.

mcp.json (mcpServers stanza)
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "demiplane": {
      "command": "demiplane",
      "args": [
        "mcp",
        "--url", "https://demiplane.example",
        "--token-file", "~/.config/demiplane/token"
      ]
    }
  }
}
Claude Code
claude mcp add, or ~/.claude.json → mcpServers
Cursor
~/.cursor/mcp.json (or .cursor/mcp.json in a project)
Cline
VS Code settings → Cline MCP Servers
Windsurf
~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json
Zed
settings.json → context_servers
Continue
~/.continue/config.json → mcpServers

Claude Code one-liner (adds the stanza above for you):

Claude Code
claude mcp add demiplane -- demiplane mcp --url https://demiplane.example --token-file ~/.config/demiplane/token

Claude Code capture hook

Prefer a hook over MCP? The companion Stop-hook publishes the transcript or a named artifact automatically. See companion/README.md in the repo; the hook targets this instance with:

companion hook env
export DEMIPLANE_URL=https://demiplane.example
export DEMIPLANE_TOKEN_FILE=~/.config/demiplane/token

Bare curl

The universal fallback. Any harness, script, or terminal that can run curl can publish:

Publish a page
curl --data-binary @index.html https://demiplane.example/publish

Add query parameters to control the result:

Variations
# stable slug that overwrites in place
curl --data-binary @report.html "https://demiplane.example/publish?slug=reports"

# private, unguessable URL that expires in a day
curl --data-binary @secret.html "https://demiplane.example/publish?private=true&ttl=24h"

# render markdown to a styled page
curl --data-binary @notes.md "https://demiplane.example/publish?render=md"

# set a view password (header, never the URL)
curl -H "X-Demiplane-Password: hunter2" --data-binary @q.html "https://demiplane.example/publish?slug=q"

Command-line client

The demiplane publish subcommand wraps the same call, copies the URL to your clipboard, and can watch a file for live-reload:

demiplane publish
# publish a file; the URL is printed and copied to your clipboard
demiplane publish --url https://demiplane.example --token-file ~/.config/demiplane/token index.html

# watch a file and re-publish on every save (pairs with ?live for auto-reload)
demiplane publish --url https://demiplane.example --token-file ~/.config/demiplane/token --watch --slug draft notes.md

# or set the environment once and drop the flags
export DEMIPLANE_URL=https://demiplane.example
export DEMIPLANE_TOKEN_FILE=~/.config/demiplane/token
demiplane publish index.html

Aider and shell-command harnesses

Harnesses that expose a shell command (Aider's /run, for example) publish through the same curl. Run it and paste the returned URL back:

Aider /run
/run curl --data-binary @index.html https://demiplane.example/publish

Fetch it back

Every publish returns a URL. Fetch it from anything on your network:

Read
curl https://demiplane.example/shadow-specter

Learn more

demiplane · your private publishing plane.
